22.7:
In my distress I cried to the LORD,
I called out to my God—
and from his temple he heard my voice;
my cry reached his very ears.
22.9:
Smoke poured from his nostrils,
devouring fire from his mouth—
glowing coals blazing before him.
22.10:
He split the heavens and came down;
thick darkness was beneath his feet.
22.11:
He rode upon a cherub and flew;
he swept in on the wings of the wind.
22.12:
He wrapped himself in darkness,
his canopy of massed cloud and storm water,
thick clouds of the sky.
22.13:
Out of the brightness before him
coals of fire burst into flame.
22.14:
The LORD thundered from heaven;
the Most High spoke in thunder.
22.15:
He shot his arrows—they scattered.
Lightning bolts—and they were routed.
22.16:
The depths of the sea were exposed;
the foundations of the world laid bare—
at the LORD's rebuke,
at the blast of his breath from his nostrils.
22.17:
He reached down from on high and took me;
he pulled me out of deep waters.
22.18:
He rescued me from my powerful enemy,
from those who hated me—
they were too strong for me.
22.19:
They came at me in my hour of collapse—
but the LORD was my support.
22.20:
He brought me out to a wide, open place;
he saved me—because he delighted in me.
22.21:
The LORD dealt with me as my faithfulness deserved;
he repaid me as my clean hands required.
22.22:
For I have kept the LORD's ways
and not turned treacherously from my God.
22.23:
All his ordinances were before me;
I did not put his statutes aside.
22.24:
I was blameless before him
and kept myself from deliberate sin.
22.25:
So the LORD repaid me as my faithfulness deserved—
my cleanness of hands before his eyes.
22.26:
To the faithful you show yourself faithful;
to the blameless you show yourself blameless;
22.27:
to the pure you show yourself pure—
but with the twisted you show yourself a match for their twisting.
22.28:
You save the lowly;
but your eyes are on the proud—to bring them down.
22.29:
For you are my lamp, O LORD;
the LORD turns my darkness to light.
22.30:
With your help I can charge a troop;
with my God I can leap over a wall.
22.32:
Who is God but the LORD?
Who is the Rock except our God?
22.33:
This God is my strong refuge;
he makes my way sure.
22.34:
He gives me feet like a deer's
and sets me secure on the mountain heights.
22.35:
He trains my hands for battle;
my arms bend a bow of bronze.
22.36:
You give me the shield of your salvation;
your stooping-down to me has made me great.
22.37:
You widen the path beneath my feet;
my feet do not slip.
22.38:
I pursued my enemies and destroyed them;
I did not turn back until they were finished.
22.39:
I crushed them; they fell—
struck through, unable to rise,
broken beneath my feet.
22.40:
You armed me with strength for the battle;
you brought down under me those who rose against me.
22.41:
You made my enemies turn their backs to me;
those who hated me—I annihilated them.
22.42:
They looked for help—no one was there.
They cried to the LORD—he did not answer them.
22.45:
Foreigners came cowering to me;
the moment they heard of me, they submitted.
22.46:
Foreigners lost their courage
and came staggering from their strongholds.
22.47:
The LORD lives!
Blessed be my Rock!
Exalted be the God of my salvation—
22.48:
the God who gives me justice,
who brings peoples down under me,
22.50:
Therefore I will praise you, LORD, among the nations;
I will sing to your name.

23.1:
These are the last words of David—
the declaration of David son of Jesse,
the declaration of the man raised to the heights,
the anointed one of the God of Jacob,
Israel's beloved singer of songs:
23.2:
The Spirit of the LORD speaks through me;
his word is on my tongue.
23.3:
The God of Israel has spoken;
the Rock of Israel said to me:
the one who rules over people with justice,
who rules in the fear of God—
23.4:
—is like the light of morning,
like sunrise on a cloudless day,
like the bright gleaming after rain
when grass springs from the earth.
23.5:
Is my house not so with God?
He has made with me an everlasting covenant—
ordered in all things and sure.
For this is all my salvation and all my desire:
will he not make it flourish?
23.6:
But men of Belial are like thorns thrown aside—
they cannot be grasped with the bare hand;
23.7:
whoever handles them takes iron in hand,
a spear-shaft—
and in their place they are burned to nothing.
23.8: These are the names of David's mighty warriors: Josheb-basshebeth the Tahchemonite, head of the Three. He faced eight hundred men with his spear and killed them all in one engagement.
23.9: Next among the three was Eleazar son of Dodo son of Ahohi. He was with David when they challenged the Philistines at Pas-dammim—where Israel had assembled for battle and the men of Israel began to fall back.
23.10: Eleazar stood his ground and struck down the Philistines until his hand was exhausted and had to be pried from the sword. The LORD won a great victory that day. The men came back—but only to plunder the dead.
23.11: Next was Shammah son of Agee the Hararite. The Philistines had gathered at Lehi, where there was a field full of lentils. The men of Israel ran.
23.12: But Shammah stood in the middle of that field and defended it and killed the Philistines. The LORD won a great victory.
23.13: Three of the thirty chiefs went down at harvest time to David at the cave of Adullam, while a Philistine force was camped in the Valley of Rephaim.
23.14: David was in the stronghold; the Philistines held Bethlehem.
23.15: David was seized by longing and said, 'If only someone would give me water to drink from the well at the gate of Bethlehem!'
23.16: The three broke through the Philistine lines, drew water from the well at Bethlehem's gate, and brought it to David. But he would not drink it. He poured it out before the LORD—
23.17: 'The LORD forbid that I drink this,' he said. 'Is this not the blood of men who put their lives in danger to bring it?' He would not drink it. This is what the three did.
23.18: Abishai, Joab's brother, was head of the Thirty. He faced three hundred men with his spear and killed them all. He won fame alongside the Three—
23.19: —the most honored of the Thirty and their captain. But he was not counted among the Three.
23.20: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a courageous man from Kabzeel, a man of great exploits. He struck down two of Moab's champions. He went down into a pit on a snowy day and killed a lion.
23.21: He also killed an imposing Egyptian—the man had a spear; Benaiah went at him with a staff, wrenched the spear away from him, and killed him with his own weapon.
23.22: These were the deeds of Benaiah son of Jehoiada. He won a name alongside the Three.
23.23: He was more honored than the Thirty, but not among the Three. David appointed him over his personal bodyguard.
23.24: Asahel, Joab's brother, was among the Thirty. Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
23.25: Shammah of Harod, Elika of Harod,
23.26: Helez the Paltite, Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa,
23.27: Abiezer of Anathoth, Mebunnai the Hushathite,
23.28: Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai of Netophah,
23.29: Heleb son of Baanah of Netophah, Ittai son of Ribai of Gibeah of Benjamin,
23.30: Benaiah of Pirathon, Hiddai of the wadis of Gaash,
23.31: Abi-albon the Arbathite, Azmaveth of Bahurim,
23.32: Eliahba the Shaalbonite, the sons of Jashen, Jonathan,
23.33: Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam son of Sharar the Hararite,
23.34: Eliphelet son of Ahasbai of Maacah, Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite,
23.35: Hezro of Carmel, Paarai the Arbite,
23.36: Igal son of Nathan of Zobah, Bani the Gadite,
23.37: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai of Beeroth—Joab son of Zeruiah's armor-bearer—
23.38: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
23.39: Uriah the Hittite. Thirty-seven in all.

24.1: Again the LORD's anger burned against Israel, and he moved David against them, saying: 'Go and count Israel and Judah.'
24.2: The king told Joab, the army commander at his side: 'Go through all the tribes of Israel—from Dan to Beersheba—and count the people. I want to know the number.'
24.3: Joab said to the king, 'May the LORD your God multiply the people a hundredfold—enough for my lord the king to see it himself. But why does my lord the king want this?'
24.4: The king's word overruled Joab and the commanders. So Joab and the army commanders left the king's presence to count the people of Israel.
24.5: They crossed the Jordan and started at Aroer, on the south side of the city in the middle of the Gadite valley, working toward Jazer.
24.6: From there to Gilead, to Kadesh in the land of the Hittites, then to Dan, then around toward Sidon—
24.7: —and came to the fortress of Tyre and all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites. They finished at Beersheba in the Negeb of Judah.
24.8: They covered the whole land and came back to Jerusalem after nine months and twenty days.
24.9: Joab reported the count to the king: eight hundred thousand fighting men in Israel who could draw the sword, and five hundred thousand in Judah.
24.10: After the census, David's heart condemned him. He said to the LORD, 'I have sinned greatly in what I have done. Now, LORD, take away the guilt of your servant. I have been very foolish.'
24.11: When David rose the next morning, the word of the LORD had come to Gad the prophet—David's own seer:
24.12: 'Go tell David: the LORD says this—I lay three choices before you. Choose one, and I will do it.'
24.13: Gad came to David and laid out the options: 'Three years of famine in your land? Or three months of fleeing from your enemies while they pursue you? Or three days of plague in your land? Think it over. What shall I tell the one who sent me?'
24.14: David said to Gad, 'I am in anguish. Let us fall into the LORD's hands—his mercy is great. But let me not fall into human hands.'
24.15: The LORD sent a plague on Israel from that morning until the end of the appointed time. Seventy thousand men died—from Dan to Beersheba.
24.16: When the angel stretched out his hand toward Jerusalem to destroy it, the LORD relented from the disaster. He said to the angel who was bringing devastation: 'Enough. Pull back your hand.' The angel of the LORD was standing at the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.
24.17: When David saw the angel who was striking the people down, he cried out to the LORD: 'I am the one who sinned. I am the one who did wrong. These are sheep—what did they do? Let your hand be against me and my father's house, not against them.'
24.18: That day Gad came to David: 'Go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.'
24.19: David went up at Gad's word, just as the LORD had commanded.
24.20: Araunah looked up and saw the king coming toward him with his servants. He went out and bowed to the ground before the king.
24.21: 'Why has my lord the king come to his servant?' Araunah asked. David said, 'To buy your threshing floor and build an altar to the LORD—so the plague on the people may stop.'
24.22: Araunah said to David, 'Take whatever you need, my lord the king, and offer it. Here are the oxen for the burnt offering, and the threshing sledges and ox yokes for the wood.'
24.23: 'All of it, my lord the king—Araunah gives it all to the king.' And he added: 'May the LORD your God receive you with favor.'
24.24: But the king said, 'No. I will buy it from you at the full price. I will not offer to the LORD my God burnt offerings that cost me nothing.' David paid fifty shekels of silver for the threshing floor and the oxen.
24.25: David built the altar there and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. The LORD heard the plea for the land, and the plague over Israel was stayed.
